title: dense-time verification (deferred tier)
manager: FSL's timing today reasons in discrete steps; dense-time verification asks the harder question where clocks run over the real line, so 'these two timeouts can never both fire within 5ms of each other' is checked as a genuine continuous-time property rather than approximated by sampling. This is the timed-automata rung of the ladder, and it is deliberately deferred: the region/zone machinery is substantial and it is the foundation the probabilistic-timed tier stands on. The item pins the scope — which dense-time claims FSL will make, which of them are decidable, how they lower to a timed-automata checker — so the deferred tier has a spec to build against and the core never over-promises real-time guarantees.
cs: timed automata with clock constraints; zone-based reachability over difference-bound matrices via a UPPAAL-class backend; reachability is decidable but too-rich clock arithmetic tips into undecidable, so scope stays inside the decidable fragment; the base layer Tfsl-1362 builds on.
